definition: Endocrine disruptors are a broad class of chemicals that may interfere with hormonal signaling (e.g., certain plasticizers, pesticides). Evidence quality varies by compound and study design; GENARCH includes this category to support educational discussion of environmental drivers in metabolic and reproductive epidemiology, without making individual-level claims.
name: Endocrine Disruptors (Broad Category)

references.title: Endocrine disruptors and metabolic outcomes: evidence overview (review)

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Developmental windows are often hypothesized to be more sensitive to endocrine perturbations in some study frameworks.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Some compounds are hypothesized to affect hormone receptor signaling and metabolic regulation; evidence is heterogeneous.
